registry_refs.notes: The registry contains two publication-scoped Digital Commons rows for /wmlr; W&L rank is 36.
Existing publication-scoped sitemap seed; it currently points to /wmlr/sitemap.xml rather than the observed all-issues archive.

publication_scope.notes: The /wmlr publication slug is the collection boundary on a shared repository that also hosts several other William & Mary journals.

evidence.notes: User-agent * does not disallow /wmlr/ or /cgi/viewcontent.cgi, but it does disallow /do/ and numerous account and submission routes. OAI and repository search endpoints must not be used.
One publication-scoped page lists volumes 1 through 67 and every visible issue link, spanning 1957-2026; no next-page control was observed.

Newest sampled issue contains seven div.doc records: one table of contents and six authored works. Each record advertises an article landing page and cgi/viewcontent file link; authored works expose title and author inline.
Middle sampled issue (1992) uses the same div.article-list/div.doc structure, article landing paths, author spans, and cgi/viewcontent links as the newest sample.

Oldest sampled issue (1957) uses the same listing structure and includes authored works plus non-article records such as a table of contents and a cumulative predecessor index, which require classification filters.

Article page exposes bepress_citation meta tags for journal, first page, repeated authors and affiliations, title, year, volume, issue, PDF URL, article URL, print and online ISSNs, and online date. The DOM also contains a multi-paragraph abstract and repository citation.

A bounded ranged GET with the article page as Referer returned 744 bytes of HTML beginning with <html>, not %PDF, and set a short-lived original_referer cookie. No retry or access-control bypass was attempted.

reconnaissance.notes: Seven sequential GETs established a complete visible archive route and a stable issue layout across oldest, middle, and newest samples. Metadata discovery is strong, but primary-file acquisition is not proven because the representative file request was blocked.

include: Issue records whose landing URLs match /wmlr/vol{volume}/iss{issue}/{article}
Articles, essays, comments, and notes attributed to William & Mary Law Review
exclude: Sibling collections on scholarship.law.wm.edu, including wmborj, wmblr, wmjowl, wmelpr, and wmlronline
Repository search, account, subscription, submission, and all /do/ routes
Tables of contents, mastheads, cumulative indexes, covers, and other front matter unless separately classified
Outbound cited documents and materials from the predecessor William and Mary Review of Virginia Law
scope.notes: Require both /wmlr/ landing-page scope and context=wmlr on cgi file URLs. William & Mary Law Review Online is a separate repository slug and is out of scope.

entry_points.notes: Preferred enumerator: publication-scoped, robots-permitted, unpaginated, and visibly spans volumes 1-67.

nodes.notes: Flat all-issues page containing volume and issue links.
Volume/issue page containing one div.doc per repository record.
Landing page with structured citation metadata, abstract, citation, and advertised file URL.
cgi/viewcontent route advertised as PDF; representative bounded request currently returns 403 HTML.

edges.notes: Issue URLs encode volume and issue; the enclosing h3 text supplies year and occasional symposium labels.
Stable across all three sampled eras; span.auth in the same record contains display-order authors when present.
Each sampled record advertises a file link, including front matter that must be classified before collection.
Structured metadata and the visible Download control advertise the same file route.

archive_patterns.notes: Volume links are present, but direct volume pages were not separately fetched because issue links are available on the archive.
Volume and issue are numeric path components.
Issue pages omit the final slash; canonicalize article landing URLs with a trailing slash.
The context=wmlr query is the publication-scope guard; current bounded retrieval returned 403 HTML.

pagination.termination: The one response ends after volume 1, issue 1; the first record in the response is volume 67, issue 1-6.
pagination.notes: No next or numbered pagination was observed. Volumes 1-67 are all represented in the same HTML document.

layout_eras.label: Unified Digital Commons issue and article layout
layout_eras.notes: Issue-list markup is stable in 1957, 1992, and 2026 samples. Only a current article landing page was sampled, so historical article-page metadata uniformity remains inferred rather than proven.

sources.notes: The article landing-link text supplies the record title across all three sampled issues.
Treat the structured article-page value as authoritative after stripping markup.

normalization: Decode HTML entities
Strip presentational HTML while preserving text
Collapse internal whitespace
title.missingness: Present on every issue-page record in oldest, middle, and newest samples, including front matter.
title.notes: A title alone does not establish that a record is an in-scope article.

sources.notes: Issue page supplies display-order author text. Empty spans mark front matter and some institutional records.
Repeated meta tags preserve author multiplicity; adjacent institution tags align by position.

normalization: Preserve repeated authors in source order
Convert unambiguous Family, Given forms to display order
Do not split free text on 'and' when repeated meta tags are available
authors.missingness: Authored works have names in all three issue samples; tables of contents and a cumulative index have empty author spans.
authors.notes: Missing author is also a useful, but not sufficient, front-matter classification signal.

sources.example: In 2022, in New York State Rifle & Pistol Ass'n v. Bruen, the Supreme Court revolutionized the Second Amendment...
sources.notes: Join all abstract paragraphs in document order.

normalization: Join paragraph text with blank-line boundaries
Collapse HTML-only whitespace while preserving punctuation
abstract.missingness: Present on the one sampled current article; issue pages do not expose abstracts and historical article pages were not sampled.
abstract.notes: Do not claim universal abstract coverage from this sample.

sources.notes: Year-only citation field. Issue headings provide a fallback for every visible issue.
Propagate the issue year to its articles only when article-page metadata is absent.

normalization: Extract a four-digit publication year
year.missingness: A year is visible for every issue in the archive; present on the sampled article meta tags.
year.notes: Prefer the article value and cross-check it against the issue heading.

sources.notes: Full online-publication date was present in the sampled article head.

normalization: Parse year/month/day
Serialize as ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DD
date.missingness: Present on the sampled current article; historical coverage was not tested.
date.notes: Retain the raw source value with the normalized date.

sources.notes: Also encoded in every issue and article path.

normalization: Strip a leading Volume label if present
Retain the numeric string
volume.missingness: Encoded in all visible archive, issue, and article paths; present on the sampled article meta tags.

sources.notes: Also encoded in every issue and article path.

normalization: Strip a leading Issue label if present
Retain the numeric string
issue.missingness: Encoded in all visible issue and article paths; present on the sampled article meta tags.

sources.notes: The sampled page exposes a first page but no bepress_citation_lastpage tag.

normalization: Store a single starting page when no ending page is supplied
Do not infer an ending page from the next article without validation
pages.missingness: Starting page is present on the sampled article; ending page is not exposed in its citation meta tags or repository citation.
pages.notes: PDF inspection may later recover a full range after file access is resolved.

normalization: Lowercase a DOI after removing any https://doi.org/ prefix
doi.missingness: No DOI field was observed on the sampled article page.
doi.notes: Do not treat the Digital Commons repository article number as a DOI.

sources.example: Tonja Jacobi and Cory Conley, Is Bruen the New Usery?, 67 Wm. & Mary L. Rev. 1529 (2026).
sources.notes: Exclude the trailing repository URL when storing the preferred print citation.

normalization: Strip presentational HTML
Collapse whitespace
Store the repository URL separately from the print citation
citation.missingness: Present on the sampled article; historical article pages were not sampled.
citation.notes: Structured fields can be assembled defensively if citation DOM markup varies.

patterns.locator: div.article-list div.doc p.pdf > a, meta[name='bepress_citation_pdf_url'], a#pdf
patterns.notes: Pages consistently advertise a direct PDF route, but the representative bounded GET returned 403 HTML even with the article page as Referer. The response set an original_referer cookie; any cookie or browser requirement remains unverified and must not be assumed.

Tables of contents and cumulative indexes share the normal file route. Classification should primarily use title patterns and empty-author evidence; :has is descriptive and need not be used by the adapter.

validation.notes: The returned body was 744 bytes of HTML beginning with <html>, not a PDF. File granularity could not be validated because no primary file was retrieved.

robots.notes: Publication and cgi/viewcontent routes are not disallowed, but /do/ is disallowed; do not use OAI, Digital Commons search, or other /do/ endpoints.

barriers.notes: The normal Digital Commons /do/ family is disallowed, so enumeration must remain on publication HTML pages.
The representative cgi/viewcontent article-file request returned HTTP 403 HTML; metadata discovery works but PDF acquisition is not yet proven.

access.politeness_notes: Reconnaissance used seven sequential GETs, at least two seconds apart after robots, with no concurrency, no /do/ requests, and no retry after the file returned 403. Recheck robots.txt before any later smoke or collection run.

verdict.summary: The publication-scoped Digital Commons archive deterministically exposes volumes 1-67, stable issue records across 1957, 1992, and 2026, and rich current article metadata, but a representative advertised primary-file request returned 403 HTML.
blockers: Representative Digital Commons primary-file GET returned HTTP 403 HTML.
Historical article landing-page metadata uniformity has not been sampled.
Digital Commons /do/ endpoints are disallowed by robots.txt.
verdict.next_action: Run a metadata-only adapter smoke from /wmlr/all_issues.html, with strict /wmlr/ and context=wmlr scope guards, then use an authorized operator/browser check to determine whether the advertised file route is publicly retrievable without bypassing access controls.
verdict.implementation_notes: Replace the broad sitemap.xml seed with /wmlr/all_issues.html and set dc_enum_mode=all_issues_only for deterministic publication-scoped enumeration; never use /do/; classify title/authorless front matter before queuing files; keep discovery success separate from file-delivery success.
